etruscan.reconstruction_method: Greek-derived alphabet adaptation + Latin/Greek transcriptions/bilinguals + internal spelling variation/syncope patterns (Bonfante/Rix/Wallace)
etruscan.scholarly_consensus: 4-vowel system secure; aspirate/tenuis contrast dominant; no voiced stops; syllabic sonorants possible (minority).
etruscan.confidence_overview: medium (high on consonants/alphabet; medium-low on vowels/exact prosody due to isolate/sparse corpus)

consonants: p/ph, t/th, k/kh (aspirates); f s ś z (affricate?); m n l r (syllabic possible); h j w; no distinct b/d/g
phoneme_inventory.notable_absences: voiced stops (b d g absent or neutralized in loans)
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: initial stress → syncope/clusters; aspirate primary contrast; possible geminates/affricates

ipa_reconstructed.sample_text: mi mulu larisal (dedicatory formula 'I [am/gave gift] of Laris')
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Consonants/alphabet high; vowels conservative placeholder; alternatives in Rix syllabic theory
ipa_reconstructed.alternatives: More syncopated later realizations; sibilant variants

etruscan.grok_imagine_guidance: Commit to: aspirated puffs on ph/th/kh; pure vowels (i e a u); no voiced stops (use p/t/k only); initial stress. Approximate: clusters/syllabic liquids (like English 'button' n); rolled r if present. Avoid: modern voicing/Italianate or forced o-vowel. Flag: aspirate clusters harsh (Tuscan-like link); uncertainties on exact timbre — use crisp/neutral for generation.
tutoring_hooks: Etruscan had NO voiced stops (b/d/g absent) — unique among neighbors!
Greek alphabet adapted uniquely (added F-symbol for new sound).
Initial stress caused vowel syncope → wild consonant clusters.
We read it phonetically far better than we understand meanings (isolate).
Influenced Latin & possibly modern Tuscan 'gorgia' aspiration.

sources.author: Bonfante & Bonfante
sources.title: The Etruscan Language: An Introduction (2nd ed.)

sources.author: Rex E. Wallace
sources.title: Zikh Rasna: A Manual of the Etruscan Language and Inscriptions

hittite.reconstruction_method: Neo-Hittite cuneiform syllabic spelling + plene vowel writing (V/VV) + comparative Anatolian (Luwian/Palaic) + Akkadian loanword evidence + PIE laryngeal confirmation via ḫ
hittite.scholarly_consensus: Melchert/Kimball standard: four vowels /a e i u/ with phonemic length; fortis/lenis (geminate vs single) stop contrast dominant over voicing; ḫ as uvular/velar fricative; no distinct voiced stops in standard model; cuneiform maps syllables reliably in Neo-Hittite orthography.
hittite.confidence_overview: medium (high on consonant inventory, ḫ, and vowel length; medium on unstressed vowel qualities, exact fortis articulation, and stress placement)
hittite.laryngeal_notes: Hittite ḫ (cuneiform ḫa/ḫi etc.) preserves PIE laryngeals *h2/*h3 as a guttural fricative [x~χ] (uvular to velar); colors adjacent vowels (e.g. *h2 → a-coloring); key empirical proof of the laryngeal theory unavailable in Greek, Latin, or Sanskrit.

vowels: /a e i u/ with phonemic length /aː eː iː uː/ (plene writing and comparative evidence); unstressed qualities partly inferred
consonants: Stops: p pp t tt k kk (fortis/lenis); Fricatives: s š ḫ [x~χ]; Nasals: m n; Liquids: l r; Glides: y w; no distinct b d g in standard Melchert model
laryngeals: ḫ = [x~χ] (PIE *h2/*h3 reflex); possible allophonic variation uvular vs velar
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: Fortis/lenis stop contrast; preserved laryngeals; vowel reduction in unstressed syllables; Indo-European's oldest attested branch

ipa_reconstructed.sample_text: namma antuhsas andan esdu (ritual formula: 'let mankind remain inside')
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Consonants and ḫ secure; vowel qualities in antuhsas/esdu reconstructed from plene spellings; stress inferred from Anatolian comparative patterns
ipa_reconstructed.alternatives: Sturtevant voiced/voiceless model; penultimate stress hypothesis; ḫ as [ħ] in older literature

hittite.stress_rules: Likely initial or penultimate stress (Anatolian comparative evidence); exact pattern disputed — conservative default: stress first full syllable of content word

prosody.rhythm: Syllable-timed with fortis geminates lengthening syllables
prosody.pacing: Formulaic ritual/legal diction; deliberate enunciation in cult texts
prosody.notes: Cuneiform does not mark stress; pacing inferred from parallel Luwian and meter in hymns

hittite.grok_imagine_guidance: Commit to: guttural ḫ as back-of-throat fricative (Scottish 'loch' / German 'Bach', not English h); fortis geminates held longer (pp/tt/kk); pure vowels a/e/i/u; no English voiced b/d/g defaults. Approximate: š as [ʃ]; long vowels from double VV spellings sustained ~1.5×. Avoid: treating ḫ as silent or as kh; Slavic-style soft consonants; Modern Turkish vowel harmony. Flag: ḫ has no English equivalent — describe as uvular scrape; geminate stops need audible hold.
tutoring_hooks: Hittite is the OLDEST attested Indo-European language (~1650 BCE) — older than Greek or Sanskrit texts.
Hittite ḫ preserved PIE laryngeals that vanished everywhere else — proof of the laryngeal theory!
Cuneiform looks like bird tracks but spells syllables: ḫa = ḫ+a, not a logogram.
Fortis/lenis (double vs single consonants) matters more than voiced/voiceless in standard reconstruction.
The Hittite Laws open with a formula every student of ancient law recognizes — in a language dead 3,000 years.

sources.author: H. Craig Melchert
sources.title: Hittite Historical Phonology (in The Hittite Language)

sources.author: Alwin Kloekhorst
sources.title: Etymological Dictionary of the Hittite Inherited Lexicon

middle-egyptian.reconstruction_method: Coptic phonological survival + comparative Afroasiatic (Semitic/Berber) + Greek/Neo-Assyrian transcriptions of Egyptian proper names + internal consonantal skeleton of hieroglyphs
middle-egyptian.scholarly_consensus: Consonants relatively secure: ejectives (pʼ tʼ kʼ), pharyngeals (ꜥ/ḥ), emphatics, and glottal (ꜣ); vowels reconstructed as /a i u/ with length contrast, heavy dialectal variation; unstressed reduction to schwa-like [ə] common in Egyptological convention.
middle-egyptian.confidence_overview: medium-low overall (high–medium on consonants and ejectives/pharyngeals; low on exact vowel timbre, stress, and dialect splits)
middle-egyptian.vowel_reconstruction_note: Hieroglyphs record a consonantal skeleton only — vowels from Coptic reflexes + transcriptions/AA cognates (high uncertainty on exact quality/timbre/dialects; schwa-like unstressed common). Egyptologists insert epenthetic vowels (e, a) by convention; these are not written in originals.

vowels: /a i u/ with length /aː iː uː/ (Coptic and transcription evidence); unstressed [ə] epenthetic; exact timbre disputed across Nile Valley dialects
consonants: Stops: p t k (plain) + pʼ tʼ kʼ (ejectives); Fricatives: f s z ḫ [x] h; Pharyngeals: ꜥ [ʕ] ḥ [ħ]; Emphatics: d (cuneiform ṭ evidence); Nasals m n; Liquids r l; Glottal ꜣ [ʔ]
phoneme_inventory.ayin_aleph_distinction: ꜥ (ayin) = voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ]; ꜣ (aleph/glottal) = glottal stop [ʔ] or vowel onset — distinct in Coptic reflexes and Semitic loan comparisons
disputed_phonemes: exact vowel qualities in stressed vs unstressed syllables
j/y and w as consonants vs vowels
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: Ejectives + pharyngeals; no written vowels; Afroasiatic consonant template; Coptic provides latest direct phonological witness

ipa_reconstructed.sample_text: m rn.f n ra (formula: 'in the name of Ra')
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Consonants and pharyngeal ra (r+ꜥ) secure; vowel insertions Egyptological convention; stress on content words inferred from Coptic and comparative AA
ipa_reconstructed.alternatives: Allen: [mə ɾan.nəf ən ˈɾaːʕa]; Loprieno dialect variants; vocalization of rn.f as [ɾan.nakʰ] in other contexts (suffix-dependent)

middle-egyptian.stress_rules: Stress likely on penultimate or final content syllable (Coptic and Afroasiatic comparative evidence); exact Middle Egyptian dialect stress partly unknown

prosody.rhythm: Syllable-timed with epenthetic vowels breaking consonant clusters
prosody.pacing: Formulaic religious/royal diction; deliberate pacing in ritual phrases
prosody.notes: Hieroglyphic writing omits vowels entirely — audio generation must use reconstructed vocalization tags

middle-egyptian.grok_imagine_guidance: Commit to: ejectives pʼ/tʼ/kʼ (sharp glottal pop after stop); pharyngeal ꜥ [ʕ] (deep throat constriction, Arabic ع); emphatic/velarized consonants heavier than plain; glottal stop ꜣ where marked. Approximate: epenthetic schwa [ə] in unstressed slots; pure a/i/u vowels without English diphthongs. Avoid: pronouncing hieroglyphs as consonant-only strings; English th for ḫ; treating ꜥ as silent. Flag: ꜥ and ejectives have no English equivalents — use Arabic/Ge'ez reference clips; all vowels are reconstructed [reconstructed] tags on screen.
tutoring_hooks: Hieroglyphs spell CONSONANTS only — every vowel you hear is a scholarly guess from Coptic!
Egyptian ꜥ (ayin) is a deep throat sound [ʕ] — not a vowel, not silent.
Coptic is the time machine: the last stage of Egyptian with full vowels written.
Ejectives (pʼ tʼ kʼ) pop from the glottis — rare in European languages, common in Afroasiatic.
The formula 'life, prosperity, health' (ꜥnḫ wḏꜣ snb) adorned every noble's tomb — consonants attested, vowels reconstructed.

sources.author: James P. Allen
sources.title: Middle Egyptian: An Introduction to the Language and Culture of Hieroglyphs (3rd ed.)

sources.author: Carsten Peust
sources.title: Egyptian Phonology: An Introduction to the Phonology of a Dead Language
